bridge provider (CCIP, LayerZero, Hyperlane, Axelar, future      providers) implements this interface. The SettlementCoordinator      sends and tracks messages through this interface without knowing      the underlying transport.      A TransportProvider is responsible ONLY for transport. It MUST NOT      perform compliance, identity, settlement, minting, burning, or      policy evaluation.      Transport-level errors should be defined in ProtocolErrors.sol once      that shared library exists. Until then, implementers should define      their own errors following the naming convention      `TransportProvider__<ErrorName>`.      Implementations MUST support ERC-165 for interface detection.      The SettlementCoordinator MUST never know which concrete provider      implements this interface.","events":{"MessageDelivered(bytes32,uint64,bytes32)":{"params":{"intentId":"Intent the message relates to.","messageId":"Provider-assigned message identifier.","sourceChain":"Source chain identifier."}},"MessageDeliveryFailed(bytes32,bytes32,bytes)":{"params":{"intentId":"Intent the message relates to.","messageId":"Provider-assigned message identifier.","reason":"Provider-specific failure reason."}},"MessageDispatched(bytes32,uint64,bytes32,address)":{"params":{"destinationChain":"Target chain identifier.","intentId":"Intent the message relates to (`bytes32(0)`                          for non-transfer messages).","messageId":"Provider-assigned message identifier.","sender":"Address that initiated the dispatch."}}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"estimateFee(uint64,(uint16,bytes32,uint8,uint8,bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64,address,address,bytes32,bytes))":{"details":"The returned fee is an estimate. estimateFee(uint64,(uint16,bytes32,uint8.details: The returned fee is an estimate. The actual fee charged at      `sendMessage` time may differ.

params.message: The message to estimate fees for.

returns.fee: Estimated native token cost of transport.

getCapabilities().details: Each bit corresponds to a `TransportCapability` enum value.      Consumers check support with      `(capabilities >> uint256(TransportCapability.XXX)) & 1 == 1`.

getProviderId().details: MUST match the `transportId` used in `BridgeMessage` so the      SettlementCoordinator can correlate messages with the provider.

sendMessage(uint64,address,(uint16,bytes.details: The provider encodes the BridgeMessage into its native format,      pays any required fees, and submits it for transport.

params.receiver: Address of the contract that will receive the                         message on the destination chain.

returns.messageId: Provider-assigned unique message identifier.                         Used for subsequent status queries.

returns.supported: True if the provider can deliver messages to the chain.

estimateFee(uint64,(uint16,bytes32,uint8.notice: Estimates the transport fee for dispatching a message.

getCapabilities().notice: Returns a bitmask of supported capabilities.

getMessageStatus(bytes32).notice: Returns the delivery status of a previously sent message.

getProviderId().notice: Returns the unique identifier of this transport provider.

sendMessage(uint64,address,(uint16,bytes.notice: Dispatches a cross-chain message through the transport provider.

supportsChain(uint64).notice: Returns whether this provider supports message transport to         the given chain.
